display.purpose: The single page headline only. Inter 800, tight tracking, two-line balance.

title.purpose: Tile names and masthead name — the scannable choosing layer.

body.purpose: Taglines and the lab note, in Soft Graphite. Short measures (<= 42ch).

label.purpose: Section-label kickers and the masthead eyebrow. Uppercase, tracked. Used sparingly.

motion.name: ease-tactile
motion.purpose: Ease-out-expo for the tile hover lift and border shift; warm and settling, never bouncy.

motion.name: duration-quick
motion.purpose: Default transition length for border-color, transform, and hover feedback.

components.name: App Tile
components.description: The signature front-door control: a row-shaped tile that launches a game or tool. Flat, drawn with a border, lifts 1px on hover.
components.html: Know MeGameA two-phone guessing duel. Find out who's really paying attention.
components.css: .ds-tile { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 13px; text-decoration: none; background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #E7E4DC; border-radius: 12px; padding: 13px 14px; transition: border-color 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-tile:hover { border-color: #D4CFC4; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-tile:active { transform: translateY(0); } .ds-tile:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #A85A1E; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-tile-icon { width: 38px; height: 38px; flex-shrink: 0; border-radius: 9px; background: #F6F5F1; border: 1px solid #EFEDE7; display: grid; place-items: center; color: #1C1B18; } .ds-tile-icon svg { width: 19px; height: 19px; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 1.6; fill: none; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; } .ds-tile-body { flex: 1; min-width: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; } .ds-tile-top { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-tile-name { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14.5px; font-weight: 650; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #1C1B18; } .ds-tile-desc { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 11.5px; line-height: 1.4; color: #57544D; } .ds-tile-chev { color: #C6C1B6; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-tile-chev svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 1.7; fill: none; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; }

components.name: Game Badge
components.description: A quiet classifying pill. Border and text share one hue; transparent fill. Green = Tool, Violet = AI-Powered. (The former Rust 'Game' badge was removed as redundant with the section header.)
components.css: .ds-badge { font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 8.5px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; border: 1px solid currentColor; border-radius: 20px; padding: 2px 7px; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-game { color: #A85A1E; } .ds-badge-tool { color: #3F6B3A; } .ds-badge-ai { color: #5B4B8A; }

components.name: Section Label
components.description: The one deliberate tracked kicker in the system: a shelf divider with a hairline rule running to the edge. Used once per section, never as an eyebrow.
components.css: .ds-section-label { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #8A867C; } .ds-section-label::after { content: ''; flex: 1; height: 1px; background: #E7E4DC; }

components.name: Colophon Mark
components.description: The hL mark: a Workshop Rust square set like a small-press colophon, not a logo lockup. The system's single accent lives here.
components.css: .ds-mark { width: 30px; height: 30px; border-radius: 8px; background: #A85A1E; color: #F6F5F1; display: grid; place-items: center; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 15px; letter-spacing: -0.03em; }

components.name: Lab Note
components.description: A dashed-border callout for 'coming next' notes. The dashed edge signals provisional — a pinned-up sketch, not a shipped tile.
components.html: The stewards have taken their seats. Next on the docket: an 8am trawl. No ETA. Watch this space.
components.css: .ds-lab { background: transparent; border: 1px dashed #E7E4DC; border-radius: 12px; padding: 15px 16px; color: #57544D; font-family: Inter, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.55; display: flex; gap: 12px; align-items: flex-start; } .ds-lab-icon { width: 30px; height: 30px; flex-shrink: 0; border-radius: 8px; background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #EFEDE7; display: grid; place-items: center; color: #8A867C; } .ds-lab-icon svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; stroke: currentColor; stroke-width: 1.6; fill: none; stroke-linecap: round; stroke-linejoin: round; } .ds-lab strong { color: #1C1B18; font-weight: 650; }

narrative.overview: hammyLabs looks like a maker's bench, not a company. The ground is warm paper, the text is ink pressed into it, and a single burnt-orange — Workshop Rust — appears like a stamp on the things worth noticing. Everything is set out on a shelf you'd be proud to show a friend: an index of games and tools built by one person, by hand, at odd hours. The mood is quiet, warm, and human — a personal front door with fingerprints on it. The design carries the personality; it never sits on top of it as decoration. It rejects the dark techy dashboard, the generic SaaS landing, and the cluttered app-store wall.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper ground (#F6F5F1), ink-on-paper text, one rust accent used sparingly.
Flat by conviction — depth is drawn with borders, never lit with shadows.
A single family (Inter) worked hard across weights, not a font pairing.
Tiles are a considered index, not a catalogue — room to breathe over density.
Voice-forward: the copy and the details are the brand.

rules.name: The One Stamp Rule
rules.body: Workshop Rust is a stamp, and the hL colophon mark is its home — one small filled square, the maker's mark, and effectively the only place the primary accent appears at rest. It never becomes a gradient, never washes across a section, and never multiplies into decoration. The two role-colours (Green / Violet) speak only through badges.

rules.name: The Warm Contrast Rule
rules.body: Pencil Gray (#8A867C, 3.3:1) is for labels and metadata. Any text meant to be read as a sentence uses Soft Graphite (#57544D) or darker. Light gray 'for elegance' is forbidden; warmth comes from the paper, not from washing out the ink.

rules.name: The Single Voice Rule
rules.body: One family, always. Hierarchy is carried by weight (400 → 650 → 800) and size. A serif display or a mono label would read as costume on this bench.

rules.name: The Paper-Flat Rule
rules.body: No surface in the system casts a shadow, at rest or on hover. Elevation is the border and the paper-to-white step, nothing else. If a component needs a shadow to read, the layout — not the lighting — is wrong.

dos: Do keep Warm Paper (#F6F5F1) as the ground and press ink into it. Warmth lives in the paper and the copy, never in washed-out gray text.
Do treat Workshop Rust as a stamp — badges and rare emphasis only, never a fill or gradient.
Do draw depth with the 1px Deckle Edge border and the paper-to-white step. Flat is the house style.
Do carry hierarchy with Inter's weights (400 → 650 → 800). One family, always.
Do keep body text at Soft Graphite (#57544D) or darker; reserve Pencil Gray for labels.
Do let the tiles breathe — an index with room, not a packed grid.
Do add a visible Workshop Rust focus-visible ring to every tappable tile.
donts: Don't build a dark techy dashboard — no neon-on-black, no terminal cliché, no clinical tool-shell.
Don't build a generic SaaS/startup landing — no gradient hero, no identical feature-card grid, no 'sign up free' polish.
Don't build a cluttered app-store wall — no dense samey tiles, ratings, or badge pile-ups.
Don't cast shadows or reach for glassmorphism to fake depth. Ever.
Don't use background-clip: text gradient headings — emphasis is weight and size, in a single solid ink.
Don't introduce a second typeface, or use mono as shorthand for 'technical'. Inter is the voice.
Don't turn the section-label kicker into an eyebrow above every element — it is a shelf divider, used once per section.
Don't let role-colours (Rust / Green / Violet) escape their badges into decoration.
